commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=afda790ca43e5dacaa5eb7cf8df4dfad14aa6854)
commands.review_summary:
The PR conditionally passes Chokidar `awaitWriteFinish` only when `VITEST` enables polling for the Gateway config reload and skills refresh watchers.

PR surface: Source +15. Total +15 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence local macOS repro was run in this read-only review. Source inspection and upstream Chokidar docs support the reported mechanism: current main enables `awaitWriteFinish` on both watchers, and Chokidar polls file size while that option is active.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human follow-up is needed because the external PR is dirty, lacks required real macOS proof, and needs a compatibility-safe watcher repair or explicit maintainer risk decision before merge.

target.title: fix: disable awaitWriteFinish polling on native FS event platforms
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #87256 — high idle CPU (60–93%) on macOS caused by chokidar's  stat() polling.

## Problem

chokidar's  internally polls files with  at the configured  **even when  is false** (i.e., on macOS/Linux with native FSEvents/inotify). Two gateway watchers use it:

| Watcher | File | pollInterval |
|---|---|---|
| Config reload |  | 50ms |
| Skills refresh |  | 100ms |

This causes ~14.6  calls per second at idle, consuming 60–93% CPU on Intel Macs.

## Fix

Only enable  when  is explicitly on (test environments via ), where atomic writes are not guaranteed. On production macOS/Linux, native FS events detect file changes without the stat() polling overhead.

## Changes

-  — conditionally apply  only when  is true
-  — same pattern

## Verification

- All 225 config-reload tests pass
- All 22 skills-refresh tests pass
- No new config surfaces added; no behavior change on the event-driven path
